Phase-35.NAME: Operator Wildcard TLS Installer
Phase-35.Goal: Make the installer accept and validate an already prepared wildcard TLS certificate so KrotPN can deploy on krotpn.xyz without reissuing certificates during every install.

OperatorIntent.item-1: The operator prepares fullchain1.pem and privkey1.pem on the RU server before running install.sh.
OperatorIntent.item-2: The installer prompts for the public domain, fullchain path, and private-key path, with krotpn.xyz defaults.
OperatorIntent.item-3: The same wildcard TLS material is used for the web panels, backend/API/admin edge, and MTProto domain/SNI expectations.

NonGoals.item-1: No automatic Lets Encrypt issuance or renewal in this phase.
NonGoals.item-2: No production shared-443 MTProto cutover without the existing M-046 live rollback gate.
NonGoals.item-3: No rewrite of unrelated deploy, VPN, billing, frontend, admin, or email flows.

DataFlow.flow-1: install.sh collects RU SSH credentials, then prompts for PUBLIC_DOMAIN, TLS_FULLCHAIN_PATH, and TLS_PRIVKEY_PATH.
DataFlow.flow-2: install.sh performs or triggers remote validation on the RU server before creating the deploy config.
DataFlow.flow-3: deploy/deploy-on-server.sh installs validated TLS material to /opt/KrotPN/ssl/server.crt and /opt/KrotPN/ssl/server.key.
DataFlow.flow-4: docker-compose.yml mounts /opt/KrotPN/ssl into nginx as /etc/nginx/ssl, while backend settings read EDGE_TLS_* env values and deploy-generated MTProto secrets.
DataFlow.flow-5: M-044 and M-046 continue to own MTProto runtime policy and shared-443 live routing; M-048 supplies trusted wildcard TLS readiness.

Steps.step-1: Add installer prompts for domain, fullchain path, and private-key path after RU SSH connectivity is known.
Steps.step-2: Add remote validation for file existence, readability, non-empty material, expiry, cert/key match, and SAN coverage for the base domain plus wildcard host.
Steps.step-3: Replace unconditional production self-signed generation with an operator-wildcard path and explicit dev/self-signed fallback only when requested.
Steps.step-4: Wire generated .env values for DOMAIN, FRONTEND_URL, CORS origins, MTPROTO_BASE_DOMAIN, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, EDGE_PUBLIC_DOMAIN, EDGE_TLS_CERTIFICATE_PATH, EDGE_TLS_CERTIFICATE_KEY_PATH, and EDGE_TLS_CERTIFICATE_MODE.
Steps.step-5: Render or validate nginx domain ownership so the canonical host and wildcard host match the operator-selected domain instead of drifting from krotpn.xyz assumptions.
Steps.step-6: Add static verification and operator-live smoke instructions for a clean VPS deploy with prepared wildcard TLS files.

Verification.command-3: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-35.xml docs/modules/M-048.xml docs/verification/V-M-048.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml && mygrace lint
Verification.command-4: operator-live: copy wildcard fullchain/key to /root/krotpn-ssl on a clean RU server, run install.sh, then smoke HTTP redirect, HTTPS frontend/API/admin, certificate issuer/expiry, and MTProto SNI readiness.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Fresh install asks for domain and certificate paths and defaults to krotpn.xyz, /root/krotpn-ssl/fullchain1.pem, and /root/krotpn-ssl/privkey1.pem.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Wrong paths, mismatched cert/key, expired cert, or missing wildcard SAN abort before docker compose changes.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Valid operator wildcard TLS material is installed to /opt/KrotPN/ssl and reused by nginx/container paths without logging private key contents.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: The generated .env advertises https://krotpn.xyz, MTPROTO_BASE_DOMAIN=krotpn.xyz, and non-empty 32-hex MTProto secret values instead of defaulting production to the RU IP or blank MTProto credentials.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-5: Redeploy preserves working cert material unless a validated replacement is explicitly provided.

Risks.risk-1: Incorrect certificate paths can break public HTTPS; validation must happen before service replacement.
Risks.risk-2: Private-key leakage through shell tracing or logs is critical; commands must avoid printing key contents.
Risks.risk-3: Supporting arbitrary domains requires nginx/domain rendering discipline so hardcoded krotpn.xyz assumptions do not drift.
Risks.risk-4: Wildcard TLS readiness does not by itself prove shared-443 MTProto routing; that remains live-gated.

AutonomousExecutionSafety.safe-local: Static script parsing, XML parsing, MyGRACE lint, and smoke scripts that inspect source text are safe to run locally.
AutonomousExecutionSafety.requires-operator: Real certificate files, DNS changes, clean VPS install, HTTPS public smoke, and shared-443 MTProto live cutover require operator readiness.

ExecutionEvidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-17: install.sh prompts for PUBLIC_DOMAIN, TLS_FULLCHAIN_PATH, and TLS_PRIVKEY_PATH with krotpn.xyz and /root/krotpn-ssl defaults after RU SSH connectivity succeeds.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: deploy/deploy-on-server.sh validates operator wildcard TLS material before compose startup, installs cert/key atomically into /opt/KrotPN/ssl, and blocks silent production self-signed fallback.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-17: docker-compose.yml supports NGINX_CONF_PATH so deploy renders ignored nginx/nginx.runtime.conf for the operator-selected domain while keeping tracked nginx/nginx.conf as the krotpn.xyz fallback.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-17: Local syntax/static checks passed for bash, Phase-35 smoke, Phase-32 edge smoke, Phase-34 stabilization smoke, domain TLS edge pytest, and docker compose config.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-5: Clean VPS deploy with real wildcard certificate files remains operator-live before claiming production closure.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-17: Reviewer follow-up resolved clean VPS openssl preflight risk, disallowed /opt/KrotPN certificate source paths, and strengthened Phase-35 smoke with dynamic validator calls.
ExecutionEvidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-17: Clean VPS live deploy exposed backend restart-loop from blank MTProto secrets; hotfix generates deploy-time 32-hex secret/salt values and adds backend blank-env regression coverage.
